title: ee.status.graph.numa_pin.v1
description: NUMA-aware graph snapshot pinning posture surfaced under `ee status --json` at `data.graph.numaPin`. Scaffold contract for bd-ldstd / bd-1prrl.3 (swarmx.4); the wiring slice consumes this schema verbatim.

platform.description: Coarse host classification for the NUMA optimization. Linux is the only supported platform today; the others fall through to a safe-by-construction heap deserialization path.

supported.description: True iff `platform` exposes the syscall primitives the optimization needs.

enabled.description: True iff the operator left `[graph.numa_pin] enabled = true`.

attempted.description: True iff the loader actually invoked the pinning path. Must be false on unsupported platforms and when `enabled=false`.

succeeded.description: True iff the snapshot blob is now pinned to the requested NUMA node. The scaffold never sets this to true; only the syscall slice does.

preferredNode.description: Either the literal string `auto` or a stringified integer node id matching the operator's preference.

populateRequested.description: True iff the operator asked for MAP_POPULATE / madvise(WILLNEED) at load time.

bytesResident.description: Bytes of the snapshot blob currently resident on the preferred node. Always 0 in the scaffold.

populated.description: True iff the loader pre-faulted the snapshot blob. Always false in the scaffold.

fallbackPath.description: Coarse strategy the loader took when the NUMA optimization could not be applied. `none` is reserved for a successful pin.

snapshotPath.description: Filesystem path of the snapshot blob the loader operated on, when known.

degradedCodes.description: Stable degraded[] codes documented in tests/fixtures/failure_modes/. Empty only when the optimization succeeded.
